title: Headache Management (Mild Symptoms)
content: Mild symptoms like headaches can mostly be treated at home. It is recommended to stay home, drink enough water, take Vitamin C, and get adequate sleep. If headache symptoms do not improve after 3 days, it is recommended to connect with the online doctor for further guidance.

title: Fever, Cold, and Flu Care
content: Mild fever, cold, or flu symptoms often occur during seasonal changes. Treatment involves staying home, maintaining high fluid intake (hydration), and taking Vitamin C to support immunity. Escalation to an online doctor is necessary if symptoms persist beyond 3 days.

title: Toothache and Oral Discomfort
content: Mild toothaches or oral discomfort can be managed at home initially. Ensure good oral hygiene and rest. If the pain is severe or persists for more than 3 days, please schedule a consultation with the medical team or an online doctor.

title: Accidents and Physical Trauma
content: Critical symptoms include injuries from severe accidents. These often lead to internal damages that aren't immediately visible. It is recommended to connect with an online doctor immediately and subsequently proceed to a nearby medical center for a comprehensive checkup.

title: Chest Pain and Heart Attack Protocol
content: Chest pain is a critical symptom that may indicate a heart attack. This is a medical emergency requiring immediate action. Connect with an online doctor right away and arrange for urgent transport to the nearest medical center.

title: Broken Bones and Fractures
content: Having a broken arm or leg is considered a critical symptom. Do not attempt to move the limb unnecessarily. It is recommended to connect with the medical system's online doctor immediately and then visit a medical center for X-rays and professional stabilization.

title: Unconsciousness and Collapse
content: If a person is unconscious or has collapsed, this is a critical emergency. These cases usually involve serious internal conditions. Connect with an online doctor immediately for step-by-step guidance while emergency services are on the way.
